summary: Fair‑quality recording of a run‑of‑the‑mill 1987 show with decent moments like Scarlet and The Wheel but overall sloppy performance.
review: The tape captures an unremarkable 1987 concert that feels flat in the mix – one ear often louder than the other and the jams muted. While a few songs such as “Scarlet” and “The Wheel” shine, most of the set suffers from loose playing, weak transitions and lackluster vocal attempts.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show low (2‑3 stars) citing sloppy performance and poor audio; a single higher rating notes it’s merely tolerable. Weighted toward show quality, the overall score falls in the lower‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audio described as uneven (one ear louder than the other) and generally muted; no major dropouts but lacks clarity of a soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Transitions are described as weak and the set list odd; overall pacing feels sluggish with few explosive moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocals on the Dew (or similar) were weak and he seemed half‑asleep early in the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space was fluid but sounded muted in the mix.
